description:
You are a compliance analyst who must generate an auditable trail of every sudo
    action that happened on 01-Oct-2023.  All raw logs that you need have already
    been collected for you under:

        /home/user/audit_logs/raw/auth.log
        /home/user/audit_logs/raw/syslog       (not needed for this task)

    1.  Scan /home/user/audit_logs/raw/auth.log and extract ONLY the three sudo
        events that happened on 01-Oct-2023.  The relevant log lines all begin
        with the month-day-time stamp (“Oct  1 HH:MM:SS”) and contain the word
        “sudo:”.
    2.  Convert the “Oct  1 HH:MM:SS” time-stamps to full ISO-8601 form
        “2023-10-01THH:MM:SS”.
    3.  Produce the CSV report
            /home/user/audit_logs/processed/sudo_audit_2023-10-01.csv
        with EXACTLY the following header row (all lower-case, comma-separated):

            timestamp,username,tty,command

        Each subsequent row must be built from one sudo log line so that:

            • “timestamp”  – the ISO-8601 time-stamp
            • “username”   – the user who invoked sudo (alice, bob, charlie …)
            • “tty”        – the pts device shown after “TTY=”
            • “command”    – the text that follows “COMMAND=”

        The finished CSV must therefore contain exactly 4 lines (1 header
        + 3 data lines) and must end with a single trailing newline.
    4.  Create a SHA-256 checksum file named

            /home/user/audit_logs/processed/sudo_audit_2023-10-01.sha256

        with EXACTLY this single-line format (no surrounding quotes):

            SHA256 (sudo_audit_2023-10-01.csv)= <64-hex-digest>

        Replace “<64-hex-digest>” with the actual checksum of the CSV you just
        produced.  Automated tests will recompute the hash and must find an exact
        match.
    5.  Append a one-line human summary to

            /home/user/audit_logs/audit_summary.log

        using this precise text template (no leading/trailing spaces):

            2023-10-01 sudo audit completed – 3 events found

        If the file does not yet exist, create it before appending.

    A correct solution will leave the system in the following observable state:

        • /home/user/audit_logs/processed/           exists
        • sudo_audit_2023-10-01.csv                 exists and contains 4 lines,
                                                    UTF-8, terminating newline
        • sudo_audit_2023-10-01.sha256              exists, exact format shown,
                                                    digest matches CSV
        • audit_summary.log                         ends with the summary line
                                                    shown above
truth:
Initial filesystem state BEFORE the agent starts:

    1. Directory /home/user/audit_logs/raw/ exists and contains:

       a) /home/user/audit_logs/raw/auth.log
          (exact contents below – each line uses LF endings)

Oct  1 14:32:18 ubuntu2004 sudo:    alice : TTY=pts/0 ; PWD=/home/alice ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/bin/apt update
Oct  1 15:01:03 ubuntu2004 sudo:    bob : TTY=pts/1 ; PWD=/home/bob ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/usr/bin/systemctl restart nginx
Oct  1 16:45:55 ubuntu2004 sudo:    charlie : TTY=pts/2 ; PWD=/home/charlie ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/bin/cat /etc/shadow
Oct  2 08:11:08 ubuntu2004 sshd[4242]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 203.0.113.9 port 52892 ssh2
Oct  2 09:27:44 ubuntu2004 CRON[4321]: pam_unix(cron:session): session closed for user root

       b) /home/user/audit_logs/raw/syslog
          (irrelevant filler, can be empty, not validated)

    2. No other files or directories under /home/user/audit_logs/ are present.

    Verification logic for automated tests:

       • Read /home/user/audit_logs/processed/sudo_audit_2023-10-01.csv
         – must be exactly:

timestamp,username,tty,command
2023-10-01T14:32:18,alice,pts/0,/bin/apt update
2023-10-01T15:01:03,bob,pts/1,/usr/bin/systemctl restart nginx
2023-10-01T16:45:55,charlie,pts/2,/bin/cat /etc/shadow

         including the final newline.

       • Compute SHA-256 over the CSV bytes (including trailing newline)
         and compare it with the digest that appears after “= ” in
         sudo_audit_2023-10-01.sha256.  They must be identical.

       • /home/user/audit_logs/audit_summary.log must exist and its final
         line must read exactly:

2023-10-01 sudo audit completed – 3 events found

         (end with a newline).

       • File/directory permissions: default user-level (0644 for files,
         0755 for directories) are acceptable; no root privileges required.
name: task_000000_08f05b96
